Checks: rdata exactly matches wdata; bid==awid; rid==arid; APB slave must be a real memory model (not ~paddr) for this test; WaR serialization guarantees write committed before read
Notes / Edge Case: Core data integrity test. Requires memory-backed APB slave. WaR serialization in new RTL guarantees ordering — read WILL see write data.

Test Name: Write then read same address - partial strobe LSB only (8'h0F)
Expected APB Transactions: WR: LSB APB write only (pwdata=0xCAFE_1234 pstrb=4'hF); no MSB write; RD: 2 APB reads; LSB returns 0xCAFE_1234; MSB returns slave initial/zero value
Checks: Partial strobe: only lower 4B written; upper 4B unchanged in slave memory; rdata reflects exactly which bytes were written; MSB lane must not contain wdata MSB
Notes / Edge Case: Partial strobe + same-address read. Verifies strobe masking propagates correctly all the way through disassembler → APB slave → assembler → AXI R channel.

Test Name: Write then read same address - partial strobe MSB only (8'hF0)
Expected APB Transactions: WR: MSB APB write only (pwdata=0xDEAD_BEEF pstrb=4'hF); no LSB write; RD: 2 APB reads; MSB returns 0xDEAD_BEEF; LSB returns slave initial/zero value
Checks: MSB-only partial strobe; lower lane unmodified; rdata reflects correct byte masking; pstrb shift correctness: wstrb[7:4] shifted to pstrb[3:0]
Notes / Edge Case: Mirror of _002 for MSB-only strobe. Verifies MSB strobe shift in disassembler.

Test Name: Write then read same address - narrow size=1 LSB half (addr[2]=0)
Expected APB Transactions: WR: 1 APB write to LSB half (paddr=0x0010_0018 pwdata[15:0]=0xABCD pstrb=4'h3); RD: narrow read returns LSB half only; rdata[15:0]=0xABCD
Expected AXI Response: WR bresp=OKAY; RD rdata[15:0]=0xABCD rdata[63:16]=0 rresp=OKAY rlast=1
Checks: Narrow write+read same address same size; only LSB APB txn for both; MSB lane zeroed by assembler; data correctly round-trips through narrow path
Notes / Edge Case: Narrow same-address write/read. Tests disassembler half-select + assembler zero-fill for narrow reads.

Test Name: Write then read same address - narrow size=1 MSB half (addr[2]=1)
Expected APB Transactions: WR: 1 APB write to MSB half (paddr=0x0010_001C pwdata[15:0]=0x5A5A pstrb=4'h3 after shift); RD: narrow read returns MSB half only; rdata[47:32]=0x5A5A
Expected AXI Response: WR bresp=OKAY; RD rdata[47:32]=0x5A5A rdata[31:0]=0 rdata[63:48]=0 rlast=1
Checks: addr[2]=1 selects MSB half for both write and read; pstrb shift (wstrb[3:0]->pstrb[3:0] for MSB txn) verified; LSB lane zeroed in assembler output
Notes / Edge Case: Narrow MSB same-address write/read. Mirror of _004.

Test Name: Burst write then burst read same base address - all beats verified
Expected APB Transactions: 8 WR APB writes (unique data per beat address); then 8 RD APB reads from same addresses; WaR serialization ensures all writes committed before reads start
Expected AXI Response: WR bresp=OKAY; each RD beat rdata == wdata for that beat address; rlast on beat 3
Checks: Per-beat address stride (base+0, +8, +16, +24); each beat independently verified; rlast only on beat 3; no beat data mixing between slots
Notes / Edge Case: Burst write then burst read same address range. Full data integrity across 4 beats.

Test Name: Multiple writes same address (overwrite) then read - reads final value
Expected APB Transactions: WR1 APB (WaW same CL: serialized); WR2 APB; then RD APB; all sequential due to hazard gates
Expected AXI Response: WR1 bresp=OKAY; WR2 bresp=OKAY; RD rdata=0xBBBB_BBBB_BBBB_BBBB (latest write wins)
Checks: WaW serializes WR1 before WR2; WaR serializes RD after WR2; read sees second (latest) write value; no stale WR1 data visible
Notes / Edge Case: Overwrite scenario: WaW + WaR chained on same address. Data integrity with two sequential writes. Tests full hazard-chain ordering.

Test Name: Read unwritten address - slave returns default/reset value (not wdata)
Expected APB Transactions: 2 APB reads; slave returns its default/reset value (0 or defined initial)
Expected AXI Response: rresp=OKAY; rdata = slave default value (not any prior write data); rlast=1
Checks: No write issued before read; rdata must reflect uninitialised slave state; no phantom data from prior tests; no FIFO residue
Notes / Edge Case: Baseline: read of fresh address. Confirms no cross-test contamination and correct slave reset state.

Test Name: Narrow read size=0 (1B) LSB-aligned (addr[2]=0 addr[1:0]=0)
Expected APB Transactions: 1 APB read at LSB half; prdata[7:0] valid; other bytes don't matter
Expected AXI Response: rdata[7:0] correct; rdata[63:8]=0 (assembler zeros unused); rresp=OKAY rlast=1
Checks: size=0 selects LSB half (addr[2]=0); assembler zeros upper bytes; only 1 APB txn issued per beat
Notes / Edge Case: Minimum-width read. Completes the size=0,1,2,3 matrix for reads.
